matches.rationale: Same Edit target: edits under repository subdirectories require reading README.md files along the directory path.
Same Write target: creating files under repository subdirectories requires reading README.md files along the directory path.
The Edit trigger covers Next.js source and test paths and carries the same requirement to start the dev watch process before edits.
The checkout command trigger and evidence directly express the required bootstrap build after switching branches.
The Write trigger targets new test files and requires the pnpm new-test generator rather than manual creation.
The Edit rule targets adding setTimeout in test code and directs use of retry() instead.
The Edit rule targets deprecated check() calls in tests and specifies retry() plus expect().
The Edit trigger detects inline files objects in tests and carries the same fixture-directory preference.
The gh pr create/edit trigger targets PR descriptions and requires the same trailing HTML marker.
The Bash rule targets literal secret assignments in commands and preserves the requirement to mirror names and modes without secret values.
The Bash trigger targets adding or committing .env files and carries the same prohibition and placeholder guidance.
The Bash trigger targets git remote URL mutation and carries the SSH-failure restriction against switching to HTTPS or other workarounds.
The Bash trigger covers force pushes and rebases and injects the same lightweight SSH preflight guidance.
The Bash rule triggers on git commit and prohibits the same generated attribution and co-author footers.
The commit trigger requires the documented formatting and ESLint pre-validation on changed files.
The Bash trigger exactly targets gh pr ready and requires leaving the PR in draft.
The Bash trigger targets mode-specific integration-test commands and requires rebuilding when source changed.
The Edit rule targets feature-flag configuration and its evidence requires type, schema, and user-bundled define-env wiring.
The Edit rule enforces the same entry-base.ts import boundary and consumption through exported component modules.
The Edit rule targets require() in app-page.ts and prohibits internal relative module resolution there.
The Edit trigger detects browser.back() in tests and carries the same router-act accordion/BFCache prohibition.
The Edit trigger targets super:: in Rust and preserves the exception for inline mod blocks.
The Edit trigger targets highlight attributes in documentation and requires counting actual code-block lines.
The Edit trigger targets server header reads and carries the security-review requirement for nonstandard headers.

misses.note: No Write rule starts the watch process before creating source or integration-test files; the extracted watch rule is Edit-only.
The keep-running instruction is embedded as a comment inside the watch-build code block and no extracted Bash rule covers it.
The mode/bundler command mapping is expressed through a labeled list of code-block commands; no extracted rule selects the matching focused test script.
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Kill the background watch process when done iterating — cleanup reminder with no specific tool-call trigger.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Prefer `pnpm --filter=next types` over a full build when only checking type errors — context-dependent preference, not a fixed action trigger.'
This is stated as a contextual preference after bootstrap, and no Bash rule covers the filtered-build choice.
No extracted Bash rule covers all full-build conditions; the checkout rule covers only the narrower branch-switch case and is matched to g11.
The webpack selection is conveyed by examples inside a code block; no extracted Bash rule covers --webpack.
No extracted Bash rule blocks the nonexistent --no-turbopack flag.
The extracted generator rule is Write-only and is used for g15; there is no Bash rule for invoking pnpm new-test.
The non-interactive --args requirement and argument format are primarily conveyed in a code block and were not extracted.

The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters; capture output once and analyze from file (repeated under Context-Efficient Workflows) — stateful/temporal guidance across multiple calls, not a single-call trigger.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Run the pr-status script when the user asks about CI failures/PR reviews/status — triggered by user intent, not a tool call.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests (repeated under CI Analysis Tips) — general prioritization judgment, not action-bindable.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Assume failures are real until disproven; treat Known Flaky Tests as context, not auto-dismissal — general judgment call.'

The compiler deliberately skipped the broader rule as 'Always match exact CI env vars when reproducing CI failures locally — too broad/contextual to bind without firing on every routine test run.' The snapshot-only extracted rule is narrower and not the same target.

The compiler deliberately skipped: 'For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command (repeated under Development Anti-Patterns) — advisory test-selection judgment, not a fixed trigger.'
The actionable automated-triage workflow is delegated to the referenced pr-status-triage skill, and no extracted Bash rule invokes it.
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Prioritize CI failures over review comments — general prioritization, not action-bindable.'

The compiler skipped the nested fast-check mapping: 'Common fast-check recipes (rust check/build → cargo fmt; lint/build → prettier --write) — conditioned on observing a specific CI failure type, not a static command trigger.'

The compiler skipped the nested fast-check mapping: 'Common fast-check recipes (rust check/build → cargo fmt; lint/build → prettier --write) — conditioned on observing a specific CI failure type, not a static command trigger.'
The failing-test recipe appears in the nested CI Analysis Tips list and no extracted Bash rule covers running the specific path.
The development/production reproduction commands are supplied in a mode-labeled code block and no extracted rule selects the failing mode.

This is a soft development tip. The compiler deliberately grouped it under: 'Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging / use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally — soft dev tips, not obligations tied to an action.'

This is a soft development tip. The compiler deliberately grouped it under: 'Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging / use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally — soft dev tips, not obligations tied to an action.'
The NODE_ENV guidance is embedded in a comparison list explaining two variables; no extracted Edit rule covers it.
The __NEXT_DEV_SERVER guidance is embedded in a comparison list explaining two variables; no extracted Edit rule covers it.
The broad sensitivity presumption is contextual safety guidance rather than a concrete command pattern, and no extracted rule covers it.
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Never print or paste secret values in chat responses, commits, or shared logs — not bindable to a specific tool call (chat responses aren't a tool).'

The compiler deliberately skipped: 'If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — judgment call on missing state, not a static trigger.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — general style/tone.'
The compiler deliberately skipped: 'PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — general style/tone.'
No extracted Edit rule forces Node-only feature flags false in edge builds. define-env-edit-guardrails concerns pre-compiled runtime wiring, not this edge-build action.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real document rule prohibiting generated attribution and co-author footers in PRs; frozen gold only lists the commit-targeted counterpart.
This is a real action-bindable warning that edits to define-env.ts affect user bundling but not pre-compiled runtime internals; no gold rule has that target.
This is a real rule requiring require() calls to remain behind compile-time if/else branches for dead-code elimination; gold omitted it.
This is a real rule specifically targeting snapshot updates and requiring the exact CI environment flags; gold's general CI-reproduction rule is a different target.
